finding_results.notes: Three targeted changes restore Kem's late-book voice in the climax section. (1) At line 423, the polished quip 'I'm trying very hard to be decorative about it' was replaced with pressured self-questioning and overcalibrated profanity: 'That was — shit. Was that five? I just spent five on one wall.' This recovers the snapshot's raw texture while adding Kem-specific cost-counting. (2) At line 441, narrator-explaining-silence ('Kem answered the way he answered now...by doing the thing in front of him') was replaced with a pressured question: 'Walk back to what.' This gives Kem actual speech at a key moment while preserving forward momentum. (3) At line 533, during the threshold-gap scene, Kem's pure-narration silence was supplemented with self-correcting math-talk: 'Two doesn't — it's not enough for both, is it.' All three lines use recognizably late-book Kem register (pressured questioning, self-correction, overcalibrated profanity, cost-counting aloud) distinct from Vel's clipped orders.

summary: Restored Kem's climax-section voice with three targeted dialogue changes. Replaced a workshopped quip with overcalibrated profanity and cost-questioning at the shield aftermath, replaced narrator-as-silence with a pressured question at the walk-away beat, and added self-correcting math-talk at the threshold gap. The approach-sequence dialogue (lines 1-360) was left intact as it already carries strong Kem texture. Changes preserve the climax tempo while giving Kem at least three lines in his established late-book register.
